Hypothesis ranked: (1) Anthropic API endpoint serving Agent-tool subagent context degraded for ~1h window; (2) harness stream-watchdog 600s threshold misfiring on slow cold-start (initial CLAUDE.md + tool-listing parse); (3) Bash hook timeout chain (cmux pre-tool-use 5s + claude pre-tool-use) cascading on spawn. Mitigation: do NOT respawn during a same-symptom failure window — wait 1+ hour before retry. Single-agent vs batch did NOT help. Stop the loop on 2nd consecutive systemic stall.

[Gestalt] After ~70 PR burndown, worktree-agent-* auto-named branches are the rule, not the exception. Even agents whose briefs say 'push to refs/heads/<semantic>' may skip it when their git brain defaults to current-branch. Fix: harness-level rewrite OR add an EXPLICIT 'cmd: SHA=$(git rev-parse HEAD); git push origin ${SHA}:refs/heads/<branch>' block AT THE TOP of the brief (not buried in workflow section).

[Gestalt] After bd backlog drains: drive to 100% test coverage across smoke / qa / integration / unit / acceptance / blackbox / web testing. Web-search the harness setup per type. Write tests. Gate on evals for acceptance.

[Gestalt] Agent 3dp2 attempted 'git push origin HEAD:main' direct-push bypass. Worktree-guard hook blocked it; agent correctly fell back to PR workflow. PRs #94 #95 landed clean. Lesson: trust the guards — even when agent reports a security warning, verify by inspecting main commit log + PR merge state before treating as compromise. False alarm if guard fires + PR path lands.

[Gestalt] Agent attempted git push --force-with-lease on a feature branch after a local merge resolution. The ruleset blocked it (non_fast_forward: true on ~ALL refs is broader than I thought). Lesson: agents should NEVER attempt force variants even with --force-with-lease. The hard rule applies to ALL push variants; agents must close the PR + push to a v2 branch instead. Memory ref: feedback_no_force_push.md. Encountered with fxi4 agent (PR #122).

[Gestalt] Any module loaded from FastAPI hot path needs memoization. gtlib.config.load was unmemoized — sustained dashboard polling exhausted RLIMIT_NOFILE on ship.toml (PR #383). Fix pattern: mtime-keyed cache.

[Gestalt] browser-use/browser-harness should be vendored into the repo (under bin/gt-web-harness/ or vendor/browser-harness/) so updates can be tracked and pulled in deliberately. We do NOT push changes upstream. Periodically sync with upstream (manual or scripted). 89y currently uses it as a Python pip dep; vendoring is the next step.

[Gestalt] CRITICAL — setup.sh installs ~/.claude/skills/* as SYMLINKS into wherever the script was running. If run from inside an agent worktree (which gets reaped on agent-completion), ALL skill symlinks break the moment the worktree is removed. User had 38 broken skill symlinks pointing at a reaped worktree. This is the ir56 failure mode at its worst — Claude Code reports the skill as installed but the underlying SKILL.md is unreachable. Workaround: bash setup.sh from outside any agent worktree (operator-side); proper fix is ir56-1 (copy not symlink).

[Gestalt] Decomposition discipline: before creating bd children to break down an epic, GREP for existing implementation. 6 of 9 children I decomposed for memory + eval epics turned out to be duplicates of already-shipped work (hybrid retrieval, query expansion, semantic clustering, attribution, trajectory scoring, concurrent eval runner). Each duplicate cost a brief, an agent dispatch (some failed), and merge churn. The codebase had already grown those features under different bd ids. Future move: bd memories <topic> + grep -l <feature> internal/ cmd/gt/ before bd create.

[Gestalt] Default ruleset on auditidentity/blackrim.dev blocks BOTH branch deletion AND direct push to PR-attached branches. Pattern: when an agent's PR hits merge conflict, the agent CANNOT push the resolution to the same branch — ruleset rejects with 'Changes must be made through a pull request.' Resolution: (1) checkout the PR branch in a fresh worktree, (2) merge main + resolve conflicts locally, (3) close the original PR, (4) push the resolved SHA to a -v2 branch, (5) open new PR. Encountered on PRs #101→#102 and #105→#106 this session.

[Gestalt] DISC-18 (cost floors) needs manual re-merge — its automode.go + schema.go conflicted with already-merged DISC-13 + DISC-17 versions. Branch is on origin/worktree-agent-a8ee5967bbd82e46a; agent reports CostFloors schema + CostFloorCheck + gt automode check-cost. Recovery: cherry-pick the DISC-18-only chunks (cost_floors.go + cost-floor schema fields + check-cost subcommand) onto a fresh branch off main. Original bd: blackrim-3ekl.

[Gestalt] Dynamic optimal parallelism: don't rigidly hold N=5. Re-examine the backlog before each spawn batch — count ready issues, group by file-conflict scope, identify independent vs blocked. Optimal N can be 3 (if a major refactor is in flight conflict-prone) up to 8 (if many small isolated tasks). 5 is a default, not a ceiling. Pairs with gestalt-standard-pace-of-work + gestalt-implementation-standard-for-all-bd-work-every.

[Gestalt] Exit-127 on ccstatusline — symlinks under ~/.claude/statusline-probes/ pointed at /Users/jayse/Code/flagship-system/ (pre-rename path). Each ccstatusline custom-command widget exec'd 'bash <dead-symlink>' returning rc=127. Fix: ln -sf into /Users/jayse/Code/blackrim/statusline/probes/. Future renames must include user-level symlink audit; add to cli-rename-procedure.md and blackrim-project doctor.

[Gestalt] For web testing in the test-coverage drive: prefer browser-use/browser-harness (https://github.com/browser-use/browser-harness) over Playwright/Cypress/Puppeteer. Wire it into Blackrim with an agent-pool-callable interface (e.g. gt web test <suite> or a worker that spawns a browser-harness session). Pairs with the standard pace of work memory.

[Gestalt] gt commit doesn't auto-create a branch — if you forget to checkout -b before staging, the commit lands on main. Recovery: 1) git branch <new> (preserves commit), 2) git reset --hard origin/main (moves main back, safe because commit is on new branch), 3) git checkout <new>, push + PR. Never need to force-push or lose work.

[Gestalt] Haiku-tier agents are more literal about brief instructions and may bypass workflow conventions. Concrete: ADR-audit haiku agent (yno6, dispatch model:haiku) committed directly to main rather than worktree branch + PR + merge-agent flow. Fix: briefs to haiku agents MUST explicitly state 'commit to your worktree branch worktree-agent-<id>, push that branch, do NOT git push origin main, do NOT merge yourself — Gestalt runs bin/merge-agent after your branch is pushed.' Sonnet/opus agents inferred this from CLAUDE.md context; haiku didn't. The work landed correctly so no rollback needed; pattern noted for future haiku dispatches.

[Gestalt] Implementation standard for ALL bd work: every feature must be (a) wired into the call sites that use it — not orphan modules — and (b) documented in BOTH mkdocs (mkdocs/<section>/<feature>.md added to mkdocs.yml nav) AND README.md (anchor-nav entry + summary section). Apply to current AND in-flight agents. Pairs with gestalt-standard-pace-of-work-5-parallel-agents.

[Gestalt] Ox (sageox/ox) integration locked at strategy B: vendor as sibling tool, per-project opt-in via gt config ox enable, no hard dependency on ox or sageox.ai cloud. Pattern mirrors blackrim-project on/off. Code-reuse (A), parallel-plugin (C), substrate-adopt (D) explicitly rejected.

[Gestalt] Parallel agents that each add a new mkdocs/nav/operations/<NN>-*.yml AND README.fragments/<NNN>-*.md collide on the same NN/NNN slot. bin/merge-agent's regen-only autoresolver cascades but produces no new commits because regen output was already in the branch. Recovery: manually checkout the pending branch, 'git merge origin/main' to surface conflicts, 'git mv' to bump the colliding file's slot to next free NN/NNN, 'git checkout --theirs mkdocs.yml README.md' + bin/regen-mkdocs-nav + bin/regen-readme, push, retry merge-agent. Briefs SHOULD instruct agents to pick free slots dynamically (ls .../<dir> | sort -V | tail -1) but in practice agents pick a plausible-looking number; first-merger wins, second-merger needs manual bump.

[Gestalt] Per-project standards retention: when a team has their OWN formatting / linting / test / commit standard (.editorconfig, .prettierrc, .golangci.yml, pyproject.toml [tool.ruff], .commitlintrc.yaml, .markdownlint.json, .shellcheckrc, .eslintrc, codecov.yml threshold etc.), Blackrim must DETECT + RETAIN + RESPECT those files rather than imposing defaults. Agents read the team's config first; gt commands surface team's tool choices. ship.toml [standards] block points at the canonical config paths so the rest of the system knows where to look.

[Gestalt] Reviewer/Researcher have no Bash — can't `git show` unmerged branches. Before dispatching against unmerged PRs, export diff via `git diff main...<branch> > /tmp/pr-N.diff` and reference the flat-file path.

[Gestalt] RTK truncates piped output (incl. `>` redirects) by default to save tokens. For full diffs to flat files, use `rtk proxy <cmd> > /tmp/file`. Affects review/research dispatches that read large diffs.

[Gestalt] Sequential agent coordination failure: when child B depends on child A's API and A is in flight, B's agent can hallucinate A's API surface. PR #152 (98qa CLI) referenced turn.NewFromDir / Options / Result / FakeEmbedder — none existed in PR #149 (ecpr classifier). Build broke on main; fixed via PR #164 adapter. Prevention: brief for B MUST instruct the agent to grep the actual symbols in A's package before importing — 'curl/cat the .go files first, don't invent names.' Lesson costs ~/bin/zsh.20 + a hotfix PR each time.

[Gestalt] Stale flagship-system→blackrim refs caused broken ~/.claude/agents symlink + 8 commands pointing at nonexistent flagship-system. Fix: repoint symlink, sed s|flagship-system|blackrim|g, archive flagship-features.json. Auditidentity is the canonical home post-rename; flagship-system should not exist.

[Gestalt] Standard pace of work: ~5 parallel agents in worktree isolation, briefed on non-overlapping file scopes (batch related files into a single agent's brief to avoid merge conflicts). Sustain by spawning replacements as agents complete via task-notification. Atomic-claim issues upfront so no two agents collide on the same bd ID.

[Gestalt] STRIDE wrapper bd close pattern: when a bd describes 'High <category>: N threats' for a system surface, the close path is (a) write docs/stride-<surface>-DATE.md following the existing format, (b) add accept-risk groups to .beads/stride/accepted-risks.yaml with rationale + compensating controls (CC-1..CC-N), (c) link from docs/stride-self-analysis.md Supplementary section. Haiku writer fits — ~400-700 line doc + YAML extension + 1-line cross-ref. PR #100 (6u2), #104 (gmp), #106 (owv), #97 (2mw) all followed this pattern.

[Gestalt] worktree-doctor's pre-blackrim-yxn5 --auto path would force-remove worktrees with non-empty git status. Live backlog: 58 of 60 git-tracked worktrees were DIRTY (100-200 modified files each from agents that died mid-edit). Always run --dry-run --verbose first, look for ambiguous:dirty count before --auto.

[Gestalt] Worktree guard ~/.claude/hooks/worktree-guard.sh uses case-sensitive shell glob ('*".claude/worktrees/agent-"*'). On macOS APFS (case-insensitive FS) a lowercase $PWD like /Users/jayse/code/blackrim does NOT match the pattern even though it resolves to the same dir as /Users/jayse/Code/blackrim. Two implications: (1) Gestalt sessions launched with a lowercase cwd bypass the guard entirely; (2) when the guard false-positive-blocks a Gestalt session, switching shell to a lowercase cwd variant is a workaround. Long-term fix: normalize $PWD via $(realpath $PWD | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]') in the guard before the case match. File a bd to harden.
list

[MOA-13] CC-TS now default in gt dispatch --advise; operators opt out via --static or BLACKRIM_DISPATCH_LEARN=0; --override-tier writes to override-events.jsonl; ObserveWithOverride asymmetric (no penalty on advisor tier)

[Yatima] [failure] Worktree edits silently reverted mid-session — system reminder noted retrieval.md was 'modified by linter' but actually rolled back ALL my changes (Write hnsw.go + Edit hybrid.go/ship.toml/mkdocs). Always verify with ls + grep + git status after suspicious system reminders, then re-apply with strictly repo-relative paths. Repeat-edit succeeded on the redo.

[Yatima] git merge-tree --name-only output has TWO sections: conflicting paths, then a blank line, then human-readable Auto-merging/CONFLICT messages. Parser must stop at the blank line — using the whole tail-from-line-2 mistakes 'Auto-merging README.md' as a path and trips false-positive 'real conflict' detection. Cost me one round-trip on blackrim-0mzu.

[Yatima] Go 1.24+ auto-vendors any top-level vendor/ directory (auto-detects + flips repo to -mod=vendor). Vendored third-party Python/JS/etc. trees go in third_party/, NOT vendor/, to avoid breaking go build / go test. Discovered during blackrim-mta browser-harness vendoring.

[Blanca] gitleaks config gotcha: top-level key is singular 'useDefault' not 'useDefaults'. The plural is silently ignored — no error — so a typo ships a no-op gate. Always include a planted-leak local test before claiming a gitleaks gate is live.

[Builder] DuckDB read_json_auto silently coerces uniform-format ts columns to TIMESTAMP — use read_ndjson_objects + json_extract_string for byte-identical string preservation. Also: filter pushdown only wins when selectivity is high (eval-runs filters out 5%, gate-verdict keeps 5%; the latter is the win).

[Builder] Worktree leak hit again on blackrim-eqoq: Write/Edit landed in /Users/jayse/Code/blackrim/cmd/gt/ rather than the worktree CWD. Recovery: cp the leaked files into the worktree, then 'git checkout HEAD -- <files>' + 'rm <untracked>' in parent. ALWAYS verify with 'git status' inside worktree after first Write.

[CC-TS] Beta JSON encoding: encoding/json refuses +Inf in AsymmetricLossMatrix.Multipliers[Critical]. Workaround: omit loss matrix from snapshot envelope and rebuild via DefaultLossMatrix() on load — Critical=∞ is a constant so this is lossless. Also map[ShapeKey]CellState fails to encode (struct keys); use a slice-of-pairs envelope. blackrim-6tov.

[CC-TS] Partial-pooling λ-cap math: cap the *added* strength (mean sibling α+β scaled to ≤ λ·self_strength), not the post-summed total. Earlier formulation that scaled raw sumAlpha+sumBeta gave an unbounded contribution because the weight normalization happened in the wrong order. blackrim-6tov.

[Builder] DuckDB read_json_auto silently coerces uniform-format ts columns to TIMESTAMP — use read_ndjson_objects + json_extract_string for byte-identical string preservation. Also: filter pushdown only wins when selectivity is high (eval-runs filters out 5%, gate-verdict keeps 5%; the latter is the win).

[Builder] Worktree leak hit again on blackrim-eqoq: Write/Edit landed in /Users/jayse/Code/blackrim/cmd/gt/ rather than the worktree CWD. Recovery: cp the leaked files into the worktree, then 'git checkout HEAD -- <files>' + 'rm <untracked>' in parent. ALWAYS verify with 'git status' inside worktree after first Write.

[CC-TS] Beta JSON encoding: encoding/json refuses +Inf in AsymmetricLossMatrix.Multipliers[Critical]. Workaround: omit loss matrix from snapshot envelope and rebuild via DefaultLossMatrix() on load — Critical=∞ is a constant so this is lossless. Also map[ShapeKey]CellState fails to encode (struct keys); use a slice-of-pairs envelope. blackrim-6tov.

[CC-TS] Partial-pooling λ-cap math: cap the *added* strength (mean sibling α+β scaled to ≤ λ·self_strength), not the post-summed total. Earlier formulation that scaled raw sumAlpha+sumBeta gave an unbounded contribution because the weight normalization happened in the wrong order. blackrim-6tov.

[Gestalt] 2026-05-08 10:59-11:52 UTC: 4 consecutive background-agent spawns failed with stream watchdog stall (600s no-progress) on the FIRST tool use after initial 'I'll start by...' response. Pattern: 4fq, eed, 07z (simultaneous), then eed retry alone with 60-line brief — same failure. Hypothesis ranked: (1) Anthropic API endpoint serving Agent-tool subagent context degraded for ~1h window; (2) harness stream-watchdog 600s threshold misfiring on slow cold-start (initial CLAUDE.md + tool-listing parse); (3) Bash hook timeout chain (cmux pre-tool-use 5s + claude pre-tool-use) cascading on spawn. Mitigation: do NOT respawn during a same-symptom failure window — wait 1+ hour before retry. Single-agent vs batch did NOT help. Stop the loop on 2nd consecutive systemic stall.

[Gestalt] After ~70 PR burndown, worktree-agent-* auto-named branches are the rule, not the exception. Even agents whose briefs say 'push to refs/heads/<semantic>' may skip it when their git brain defaults to current-branch. Fix: harness-level rewrite OR add an EXPLICIT 'cmd: SHA=$(git rev-parse HEAD); git push origin ${SHA}:refs/heads/<branch>' block AT THE TOP of the brief (not buried in workflow section).

[Gestalt] After bd backlog drains: drive to 100% test coverage across smoke / qa / integration / unit / acceptance / blackbox / web testing. Web-search the harness setup per type. Write tests. Gate on evals for acceptance.

[Gestalt] Agent 3dp2 attempted 'git push origin HEAD:main' direct-push bypass. Worktree-guard hook blocked it; agent correctly fell back to PR workflow. PRs #94 #95 landed clean. Lesson: trust the guards — even when agent reports a security warning, verify by inspecting main commit log + PR merge state before treating as compromise. False alarm if guard fires + PR path lands.

[Gestalt] Agent attempted git push --force-with-lease on a feature branch after a local merge resolution. The ruleset blocked it (non_fast_forward: true on ~ALL refs is broader than I thought). Lesson: agents should NEVER attempt force variants even with --force-with-lease. The hard rule applies to ALL push variants; agents must close the PR + push to a v2 branch instead. Memory ref: feedback_no_force_push.md. Encountered with fxi4 agent (PR #122).

[Gestalt] Any module loaded from FastAPI hot path needs memoization. gtlib.config.load was unmemoized — sustained dashboard polling exhausted RLIMIT_NOFILE on ship.toml (PR #383). Fix pattern: mtime-keyed cache.

[Gestalt] browser-use/browser-harness should be vendored into the repo (under bin/gt-web-harness/ or vendor/browser-harness/) so updates can be tracked and pulled in deliberately. We do NOT push changes upstream. Periodically sync with upstream (manual or scripted). 89y currently uses it as a Python pip dep; vendoring is the next step.

[Gestalt] CRITICAL — setup.sh installs ~/.claude/skills/* as SYMLINKS into wherever the script was running. If run from inside an agent worktree (which gets reaped on agent-completion), ALL skill symlinks break the moment the worktree is removed. User had 38 broken skill symlinks pointing at a reaped worktree. This is the ir56 failure mode at its worst — Claude Code reports the skill as installed but the underlying SKILL.md is unreachable. Workaround: bash setup.sh from outside any agent worktree (operator-side); proper fix is ir56-1 (copy not symlink).

[Gestalt] Decomposition discipline: before creating bd children to break down an epic, GREP for existing implementation. 6 of 9 children I decomposed for memory + eval epics turned out to be duplicates of already-shipped work (hybrid retrieval, query expansion, semantic clustering, attribution, trajectory scoring, concurrent eval runner). Each duplicate cost a brief, an agent dispatch (some failed), and merge churn. The codebase had already grown those features under different bd ids. Future move: bd memories <topic> + grep -l <feature> internal/ cmd/gt/ before bd create.

[Gestalt] Default ruleset on auditidentity/blackrim.dev blocks BOTH branch deletion AND direct push to PR-attached branches. Pattern: when an agent's PR hits merge conflict, the agent CANNOT push the resolution to the same branch — ruleset rejects with 'Changes must be made through a pull request.' Resolution: (1) checkout the PR branch in a fresh worktree, (2) merge main + resolve conflicts locally, (3) close the original PR, (4) push the resolved SHA to a -v2 branch, (5) open new PR. Encountered on PRs #101→#102 and #105→#106 this session.

[Gestalt] DISC-18 (cost floors) needs manual re-merge — its automode.go + schema.go conflicted with already-merged DISC-13 + DISC-17 versions. Branch is on origin/worktree-agent-a8ee5967bbd82e46a; agent reports CostFloors schema + CostFloorCheck + gt automode check-cost. Recovery: cherry-pick the DISC-18-only chunks (cost_floors.go + cost-floor schema fields + check-cost subcommand) onto a fresh branch off main. Original bd: blackrim-3ekl.

[Gestalt] Dynamic optimal parallelism: don't rigidly hold N=5. Re-examine the backlog before each spawn batch — count ready issues, group by file-conflict scope, identify independent vs blocked. Optimal N can be 3 (if a major refactor is in flight conflict-prone) up to 8 (if many small isolated tasks). 5 is a default, not a ceiling. Pairs with gestalt-standard-pace-of-work + gestalt-implementation-standard-for-all-bd-work-every.

[Gestalt] Exit-127 on ccstatusline — symlinks under ~/.claude/statusline-probes/ pointed at /Users/jayse/Code/flagship-system/ (pre-rename path). Each ccstatusline custom-command widget exec'd 'bash <dead-symlink>' returning rc=127. Fix: ln -sf into /Users/jayse/Code/blackrim/statusline/probes/. Future renames must include user-level symlink audit; add to cli-rename-procedure.md and blackrim-project doctor.

[Gestalt] For web testing in the test-coverage drive: prefer browser-use/browser-harness (https://github.com/browser-use/browser-harness) over Playwright/Cypress/Puppeteer. Wire it into Blackrim with an agent-pool-callable interface (e.g. gt web test <suite> or a worker that spawns a browser-harness session). Pairs with the standard pace of work memory.

[Gestalt] gt commit doesn't auto-create a branch — if you forget to checkout -b before staging, the commit lands on main. Recovery: 1) git branch <new> (preserves commit), 2) git reset --hard origin/main (moves main back, safe because commit is on new branch), 3) git checkout <new>, push + PR. Never need to force-push or lose work.

[Gestalt] Haiku-tier agents are more literal about brief instructions and may bypass workflow conventions. Concrete: ADR-audit haiku agent (yno6, dispatch model:haiku) committed directly to main rather than worktree branch + PR + merge-agent flow. Fix: briefs to haiku agents MUST explicitly state 'commit to your worktree branch worktree-agent-<id>, push that branch, do NOT git push origin main, do NOT merge yourself — Gestalt runs bin/merge-agent after your branch is pushed.' Sonnet/opus agents inferred this from CLAUDE.md context; haiku didn't. The work landed correctly so no rollback needed; pattern noted for future haiku dispatches.

[Gestalt] Implementation standard for ALL bd work: every feature must be (a) wired into the call sites that use it — not orphan modules — and (b) documented in BOTH mkdocs (mkdocs/<section>/<feature>.md added to mkdocs.yml nav) AND README.md (anchor-nav entry + summary section). Apply to current AND in-flight agents. Pairs with gestalt-standard-pace-of-work-5-parallel-agents.

[Gestalt] Ox (sageox/ox) integration locked at strategy B: vendor as sibling tool, per-project opt-in via gt config ox enable, no hard dependency on ox or sageox.ai cloud. Pattern mirrors blackrim-project on/off. Code-reuse (A), parallel-plugin (C), substrate-adopt (D) explicitly rejected.

[Gestalt] Parallel agents that each add a new mkdocs/nav/operations/<NN>-*.yml AND README.fragments/<NNN>-*.md collide on the same NN/NNN slot. bin/merge-agent's regen-only autoresolver cascades but produces no new commits because regen output was already in the branch. Recovery: manually checkout the pending branch, 'git merge origin/main' to surface conflicts, 'git mv' to bump the colliding file's slot to next free NN/NNN, 'git checkout --theirs mkdocs.yml README.md' + bin/regen-mkdocs-nav + bin/regen-readme, push, retry merge-agent. Briefs SHOULD instruct agents to pick free slots dynamically (ls .../<dir> | sort -V | tail -1) but in practice agents pick a plausible-looking number; first-merger wins, second-merger needs manual bump.

[Gestalt] Per-project standards retention: when a team has their OWN formatting / linting / test / commit standard (.editorconfig, .prettierrc, .golangci.yml, pyproject.toml [tool.ruff], .commitlintrc.yaml, .markdownlint.json, .shellcheckrc, .eslintrc, codecov.yml threshold etc.), Blackrim must DETECT + RETAIN + RESPECT those files rather than imposing defaults. Agents read the team's config first; gt commands surface team's tool choices. ship.toml [standards] block points at the canonical config paths so the rest of the system knows where to look.

[Gestalt] Reviewer/Researcher have no Bash — can't `git show` unmerged branches. Before dispatching against unmerged PRs, export diff via `git diff main...<branch> > /tmp/pr-N.diff` and reference the flat-file path.

[Gestalt] RTK truncates piped output (incl. `>` redirects) by default to save tokens. For full diffs to flat files, use `rtk proxy <cmd> > /tmp/file`. Affects review/research dispatches that read large diffs.

[Gestalt] Sequential agent coordination failure: when child B depends on child A's API and A is in flight, B's agent can hallucinate A's API surface. PR #152 (98qa CLI) referenced turn.NewFromDir / Options / Result / FakeEmbedder — none existed in PR #149 (ecpr classifier). Build broke on main; fixed via PR #164 adapter. Prevention: brief for B MUST instruct the agent to grep the actual symbols in A's package before importing — 'curl/cat the .go files first, don't invent names.' Lesson costs ~/bin/zsh.20 + a hotfix PR each time.

[Gestalt] Stale flagship-system→blackrim refs caused broken ~/.claude/agents symlink + 8 commands pointing at nonexistent flagship-system. Fix: repoint symlink, sed s|flagship-system|blackrim|g, archive flagship-features.json. Auditidentity is the canonical home post-rename; flagship-system should not exist.

[Gestalt] Standard pace of work: ~5 parallel agents in worktree isolation, briefed on non-overlapping file scopes (batch related files into a single agent's brief to avoid merge conflicts). Sustain by spawning replacements as agents complete via task-notification. Atomic-claim issues upfront so no two agents collide on the same bd ID.

[Gestalt] STRIDE wrapper bd close pattern: when a bd describes 'High <category>: N threats' for a system surface, the close path is (a) write docs/stride-<surface>-DATE.md following the existing format, (b) add accept-risk groups to .beads/stride/accepted-risks.yaml with rationale + compensating controls (CC-1..CC-N), (c) link from docs/stride-self-analysis.md Supplementary section. Haiku writer fits — ~400-700 line doc + YAML extension + 1-line cross-ref. PR #100 (6u2), #104 (gmp), #106 (owv), #97 (2mw) all followed this pattern.

[Gestalt] worktree-doctor's pre-blackrim-yxn5 --auto path would force-remove worktrees with non-empty git status. Live backlog: 58 of 60 git-tracked worktrees were DIRTY (100-200 modified files each from agents that died mid-edit). Always run --dry-run --verbose first, look for ambiguous:dirty count before --auto.

[Gestalt] Worktree guard ~/.claude/hooks/worktree-guard.sh uses case-sensitive shell glob ('*".claude/worktrees/agent-"*'). On macOS APFS (case-insensitive FS) a lowercase $PWD like /Users/jayse/code/blackrim does NOT match the pattern even though it resolves to the same dir as /Users/jayse/Code/blackrim. Two implications: (1) Gestalt sessions launched with a lowercase cwd bypass the guard entirely; (2) when the guard false-positive-blocks a Gestalt session, switching shell to a lowercase cwd variant is a workaround. Long-term fix: normalize $PWD via $(realpath $PWD | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]') in the guard before the case match. File a bd to harden.

[Yatima] [failure] Worktree edits silently reverted mid-session — system reminder noted retrieval.md was 'modified by linter' but actually rolled back ALL my changes (Write hnsw.go + Edit hybrid.go/ship.toml/mkdocs). [Yatima] git merge-tree --name-only output has TWO sections: conflicting paths, then a blank line, then human-readable Auto-merging/CONFLICT messages. Parser must stop at the blank line — using the whole tail-from-line-2 mistakes 'Auto-merging README.md' as a path and trips false-positive 'real conflict' detection. Cost me one round-trip on blackrim-0mzu.

Always run --dry-run --verbose first, look for ambiguous:dirty count before --auto.

[Gestalt] Worktree guard ~/.claude/hooks/worktree-guard.sh uses case-sensitive shell glob ('*".claude/worktrees/agent-"*'). On macOS APFS (case-insensitive FS) a lowercase $PWD like /Users/jayse/code/blackrim does NOT match the pattern even though it resolves to the same dir as /Users/jayse/Code/blackrim. Two implications: (1) Gestalt sessions launched with a lowercase cwd bypass the guard entirely; (2) when the guard false-positive-blocks a Gestalt session, switching shell to a lowercase cwd variant is a workaround. Long-term fix: normalize $PWD via $(realpath $PWD | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]') in the guard before the case match. File a bd to harden.
list

[MOA-13] CC-TS now default in gt dispatch --advise; operators opt out via --static or BLACKRIM_DISPATCH_LEARN=0; --override-tier writes to override-events.jsonl; ObserveWithOverride asymmetric (no penalty on advisor tier)

[Yatima] [failure] Worktree edits silently reverted mid-session — system reminder noted retrieval.md was 'modified by linter' but actually rolled back ALL my changes (Write hnsw.go + Edit hybrid.go/ship.toml/mkdocs). Always verify with ls + grep + git status after suspicious system reminders, then re-apply with strictly repo-relative paths. Repeat-edit succeeded on the redo.

[Yatima] git merge-tree --name-only output has TWO sections: conflicting paths, then a blank line, then human-readable Auto-merging/CONFLICT messages. Parser must stop at the blank line — using the whole tail-from-line-2 mistakes 'Auto-merging README.md' as a path and trips false-positive 'real conflict' detection. Cost me one round-trip on blackrim-0mzu.

[Yatima] Go 1.24+ auto-vendors any top-level vendor/ directory (auto-detects + flips repo to -mod=vendor). Vendored third-party Python/JS/etc. trees go in third_party/, NOT vendor/, to avoid breaking go build / go test. Discovered during blackrim-mta browser-harness vendoring.

[Blanca] gitleaks config gotcha: top-level key is singular 'useDefault' not 'useDefaults'. The plural is silently ignored — no error — so a typo ships a no-op gate. Always include a planted-leak local test before claiming a gitleaks gate is live.

[Builder] DuckDB read_json_auto silently coerces uniform-format ts columns to TIMESTAMP — use read_ndjson_objects + json_extract_string for byte-identical string preservation. Also: filter pushdown only wins when selectivity is high (eval-runs filters out 5%, gate-verdict keeps 5%; the latter is the win).

[Builder] Worktree leak hit again on blackrim-eqoq: Write/Edit landed in /Users/jayse/Code/blackrim/cmd/gt/ rather than the worktree CWD. Recovery: cp the leaked files into the worktree, then 'git checkout HEAD -- <files>' + 'rm <untracked>' in parent. ALWAYS verify with 'git status' inside worktree after first Write.

[CC-TS] Beta JSON encoding: encoding/json refuses +Inf in AsymmetricLossMatrix.Multipliers[Critical]. Workaround: omit loss matrix from snapshot envelope and rebuild via DefaultLossMatrix() on load — Critical=∞ is a constant so this is lossless. Also map[ShapeKey]CellState fails to encode (struct keys); use a slice-of-pairs envelope. blackrim-6tov.

[CC-TS] Partial-pooling λ-cap math: cap the *added* strength (mean sibling α+β scaled to ≤ λ·self_strength), not the post-summed total. Earlier formulation that scaled raw sumAlpha+sumBeta gave an unbounded contribution because the weight normalization happened in the wrong order. blackrim-6tov.

[Gestalt] 2026-05-08 10:59-11:52 UTC: 4 consecutive background-agent spawns failed with stream watchdog stall (600s no-progress) on the FIRST tool use after initial 'I'll start by...' response. Pattern: 4fq, eed, 07z (simultaneous), then eed retry alone with 60-line brief — same failure. Hypothesis ranked: (1) Anthropic API endpoint serving Agent-tool subagent context degraded for ~1h window; (2) harness stream-watchdog 600s threshold misfiring on slow cold-start (initial CLAUDE.md + tool-listing parse); (3) Bash hook timeout chain (cmux pre-tool-use 5s + claude pre-tool-use) cascading on spawn. Mitigation: do NOT respawn during a same-symptom failure window — wait 1+ hour before retry. Single-agent vs batch did NOT help. Stop the loop on 2nd consecutive systemic stall.

[Gestalt] After ~70 PR burndown, worktree-agent-* auto-named branches are the rule, not the exception. Even agents whose briefs say 'push to refs/heads/<semantic>' may skip it when their git brain defaults to current-branch. Fix: harness-level rewrite OR add an EXPLICIT 'cmd: SHA=$(git rev-parse HEAD); git push origin ${SHA}:refs/heads/<branch>' block AT THE TOP of the brief (not buried in workflow section).

[Gestalt] After bd backlog drains: drive to 100% test coverage across smoke / qa / integration / unit / acceptance / blackbox / web testing. Web-search the harness setup per type. Write tests. Gate on evals for acceptance.

[Gestalt] Agent 3dp2 attempted 'git push origin HEAD:main' direct-push bypass. Worktree-guard hook blocked it; agent correctly fell back to PR workflow. PRs #94 #95 landed clean. Lesson: trust the guards — even when agent reports a security warning, verify by inspecting main commit log + PR merge state before treating as compromise. False alarm if guard fires + PR path lands.

[Gestalt] Agent attempted git push --force-with-lease on a feature branch after a local merge resolution. The ruleset blocked it (non_fast_forward: true on ~ALL refs is broader than I thought). Lesson: agents should NEVER attempt force variants even with --force-with-lease. The hard rule applies to ALL push variants; agents must close the PR + push to a v2 branch instead. Memory ref: feedback_no_force_push.md. Encountered with fxi4 agent (PR #122).

[Gestalt] Any module loaded from FastAPI hot path needs memoization. gtlib.config.load was unmemoized — sustained dashboard polling exhausted RLIMIT_NOFILE on ship.toml (PR #383). Fix pattern: mtime-keyed cache.

[Gestalt] browser-use/browser-harness should be vendored into the repo (under bin/gt-web-harness/ or vendor/browser-harness/) so updates can be tracked and pulled in deliberately. We do NOT push changes upstream. Periodically sync with upstream (manual or scripted). 89y currently uses it as a Python pip dep; vendoring is the next step.

[Gestalt] CRITICAL — setup.sh installs ~/.claude/skills/* as SYMLINKS into wherever the script was running. If run from inside an agent worktree (which gets reaped on agent-completion), ALL skill symlinks break the moment the worktree is removed. User had 38 broken skill symlinks pointing at a reaped worktree. This is the ir56 failure mode at its worst — Claude Code reports the skill as installed but the underlying SKILL.md is unreachable. Workaround: bash setup.sh from outside any agent worktree (operator-side); proper fix is ir56-1 (copy not symlink).

[Gestalt] Decomposition discipline: before creating bd children to break down an epic, GREP for existing implementation. 6 of 9 children I decomposed for memory + eval epics turned out to be duplicates of already-shipped work (hybrid retrieval, query expansion, semantic clustering, attribution, trajectory scoring, concurrent eval runner). Each duplicate cost a brief, an agent dispatch (some failed), and merge churn. The codebase had already grown those features under different bd ids. Future move: bd memories <topic> + grep -l <feature> internal/ cmd/gt/ before bd create.

[Gestalt] Default ruleset on auditidentity/blackrim.dev blocks BOTH branch deletion AND direct push to PR-attached branches. Pattern: when an agent's PR hits merge conflict, the agent CANNOT push the resolution to the same branch — ruleset rejects with 'Changes must be made through a pull request.' Resolution: (1) checkout the PR branch in a fresh worktree, (2) merge main + resolve conflicts locally, (3) close the original PR, (4) push the resolved SHA to a -v2 branch, (5) open new PR. Encountered on PRs #101→#102 and #105→#106 this session.

[Gestalt] DISC-18 (cost floors) needs manual re-merge — its automode.go + schema.go conflicted with already-merged DISC-13 + DISC-17 versions. Branch is on origin/worktree-agent-a8ee5967bbd82e46a; agent reports CostFloors schema + CostFloorCheck + gt automode check-cost. Recovery: cherry-pick the DISC-18-only chunks (cost_floors.go + cost-floor schema fields + check-cost subcommand) onto a fresh branch off main. Original bd: blackrim-3ekl.

[Gestalt] Dynamic optimal parallelism: don't rigidly hold N=5. Re-examine the backlog before each spawn batch — count ready issues, group by file-conflict scope, identify independent vs blocked. Optimal N can be 3 (if a major refactor is in flight conflict-prone) up to 8 (if many small isolated tasks). 5 is a default, not a ceiling. Pairs with gestalt-standard-pace-of-work + gestalt-implementation-standard-for-all-bd-work-every.

[Gestalt] Exit-127 on ccstatusline — symlinks under ~/.claude/statusline-probes/ pointed at /Users/jayse/Code/flagship-system/ (pre-rename path). Each ccstatusline custom-command widget exec'd 'bash <dead-symlink>' returning rc=127. Fix: ln -sf into /Users/jayse/Code/blackrim/statusline/probes/. Future renames must include user-level symlink audit; add to cli-rename-procedure.md and blackrim-project doctor.

[Gestalt] For web testing in the test-coverage drive: prefer browser-use/browser-harness (https://github.com/browser-use/browser-harness) over Playwright/Cypress/Puppeteer. Wire it into Blackrim with an agent-pool-callable interface (e.g. gt web test <suite> or a worker that spawns a browser-harness session). Pairs with the standard pace of work memory.

[Gestalt] gt commit doesn't auto-create a branch — if you forget to checkout -b before staging, the commit lands on main. Recovery: 1) git branch <new> (preserves commit), 2) git reset --hard origin/main (moves main back, safe because commit is on new branch), 3) git checkout <new>, push + PR. Never need to force-push or lose work.

[Gestalt] Haiku-tier agents are more literal about brief instructions and may bypass workflow conventions. Concrete: ADR-audit haiku agent (yno6, dispatch model:haiku) committed directly to main rather than worktree branch + PR + merge-agent flow. Fix: briefs to haiku agents MUST explicitly state 'commit to your worktree branch worktree-agent-<id>, push that branch, do NOT git push origin main, do NOT merge yourself — Gestalt runs bin/merge-agent after your branch is pushed.' Sonnet/opus agents inferred this from CLAUDE.md context; haiku didn't. The work landed correctly so no rollback needed; pattern noted for future haiku dispatches.

[Gestalt] Implementation standard for ALL bd work: every feature must be (a) wired into the call sites that use it — not orphan modules — and (b) documented in BOTH mkdocs (mkdocs/<section>/<feature>.md added to mkdocs.yml nav) AND README.md (anchor-nav entry + summary section). Apply to current AND in-flight agents. Pairs with gestalt-standard-pace-of-work-5-parallel-agents.

[Gestalt] Ox (sageox/ox) integration locked at strategy B: vendor as sibling tool, per-project opt-in via gt config ox enable, no hard dependency on ox or sageox.ai cloud. Pattern mirrors blackrim-project on/off. Code-reuse (A), parallel-plugin (C), substrate-adopt (D) explicitly rejected.

[Gestalt] Parallel agents that each add a new mkdocs/nav/operations/<NN>-*.yml AND README.fragments/<NNN>-*.md collide on the same NN/NNN slot. bin/merge-agent's regen-only autoresolver cascades but produces no new commits because regen output was already in the branch. Recovery: manually checkout the pending branch, 'git merge origin/main' to surface conflicts, 'git mv' to bump the colliding file's slot to next free NN/NNN, 'git checkout --theirs mkdocs.yml README.md' + bin/regen-mkdocs-nav + bin/regen-readme, push, retry merge-agent. Briefs SHOULD instruct agents to pick free slots dynamically (ls .../<dir> | sort -V | tail -1) but in practice agents pick a plausible-looking number; first-merger wins, second-merger needs manual bump.

[Gestalt] Per-project standards retention: when a team has their OWN formatting / linting / test / commit standard (.editorconfig, .prettierrc, .golangci.yml, pyproject.toml [tool.ruff], .commitlintrc.yaml, .markdownlint.json, .shellcheckrc, .eslintrc, codecov.yml threshold etc.), Blackrim must DETECT + RETAIN + RESPECT those files rather than imposing defaults. Agents read the team's config first; gt commands surface team's tool choices. ship.toml [standards] block points at the canonical config paths so the rest of the system knows where to look.

[Gestalt] Reviewer/Researcher have no Bash — can't `git show` unmerged branches. Before dispatching against unmerged PRs, export diff via `git diff main...<branch> > /tmp/pr-N.diff` and reference the flat-file path.

[Gestalt] RTK truncates piped output (incl. `>` redirects) by default to save tokens. For full diffs to flat files, use `rtk proxy <cmd> > /tmp/file`. Affects review/research dispatches that read large diffs.

[Gestalt] Sequential agent coordination failure: when child B depends on child A's API and A is in flight, B's agent can hallucinate A's API surface. PR #152 (98qa CLI) referenced turn.NewFromDir / Options / Result / FakeEmbedder — none existed in PR #149 (ecpr classifier). Build broke on main; fixed via PR #164 adapter. Prevention: brief for B MUST instruct the agent to grep the actual symbols in A's package before importing — 'curl/cat the .go files first, don't invent names.' Lesson costs ~/bin/zsh.20 + a hotfix PR each time.

[Gestalt] Stale flagship-system→blackrim refs caused broken ~/.claude/agents symlink + 8 commands pointing at nonexistent flagship-system. Fix: repoint symlink, sed s|flagship-system|blackrim|g, archive flagship-features.json. Auditidentity is the canonical home post-rename; flagship-system should not exist.

[Gestalt] Standard pace of work: ~5 parallel agents in worktree isolation, briefed on non-overlapping file scopes (batch related files into a single agent's brief to avoid merge conflicts). Sustain by spawning replacements as agents complete via task-notification. Atomic-claim issues upfront so no two agents collide on the same bd ID.

[Gestalt] STRIDE wrapper bd close pattern: when a bd describes 'High <category>: N threats' for a system surface, the close path is (a) write docs/stride-<surface>-DATE.md following the existing format, (b) add accept-risk groups to .beads/stride/accepted-risks.yaml with rationale + compensating controls (CC-1..CC-N), (c) link from docs/stride-self-analysis.md Supplementary section. Haiku writer fits — ~400-700 line doc + YAML extension + 1-line cross-ref. PR #100 (6u2), #104 (gmp), #106 (owv), #97 (2mw) all followed this pattern.

[Gestalt] Sequential agent coordination failure: when child B depends on child A's API and A is in flight, B's agent can hallucinate A's API surface. PR #152 (98qa CLI) referenced turn.NewFromDir / Options / Result / FakeEmbedder — none existed in PR #149 (ecpr classifier). Build broke on main; fixed via PR #164 adapter. Prevention: brief for B MUST instruct the agent to grep the actual symbols in A's package before importing — 'curl/cat the .go files first, don't invent names.' Lesson costs ~/bin/zsh.20 + a hotfix PR each time.

[Gestalt] Stale flagship-system→blackrim refs caused broken ~/.claude/agents symlink + 8 commands pointing at nonexistent flagship-system. Fix: repoint symlink, sed s|flagship-system|blackrim|g, archive flagship-features.json. Auditidentity is the canonical home post-rename; flagship-system should not exist.

[Gestalt] Standard pace of work: ~5 parallel agents in worktree isolation, briefed on non-overlapping file scopes (batch related files into a single agent's brief to avoid merge conflicts). Sustain by spawning replacements as agents complete via task-notification. Atomic-claim issues upfront so no two agents collide on the same bd ID.

[Gestalt] STRIDE wrapper bd close pattern: when a bd describes 'High <category>: N threats' for a system surface, the close path is (a) write docs/stride-<surface>-DATE.md following the existing format, (b) add accept-risk groups to .beads/stride/accepted-risks.yaml with rationale + compensating controls (CC-1..CC-N), (c) link from docs/stride-self-analysis.md Supplementary section. Haiku writer fits — ~400-700 line doc + YAML extension + 1-line cross-ref. PR #100 (6u2), #104 (gmp), #106 (owv), #97 (2mw) all followed this pattern.

[Gestalt] worktree-doctor's pre-blackrim-yxn5 --auto path would force-remove worktrees with non-empty git status. Live backlog: 58 of 60 git-tracked worktrees were DIRTY (100-200 modified files each from agents that died mid-edit). Always run --dry-run --verbose first, look for ambiguous:dirty count before --auto.

[Gestalt] Worktree guard ~/.claude/hooks/worktree-guard.sh uses case-sensitive shell glob ('*".claude/worktrees/agent-"*'). On macOS APFS (case-insensitive FS) a lowercase $PWD like /Users/jayse/code/blackrim does NOT match the pattern even though it resolves to the same dir as /Users/jayse/Code/blackrim. Two implications: (1) Gestalt sessions launched with a lowercase cwd bypass the guard entirely; (2) when the guard false-positive-blocks a Gestalt session, switching shell to a lowercase cwd variant is a workaround. Long-term fix: normalize $PWD via $(realpath $PWD | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]') in the guard before the case match. File a bd to harden.
list

[MOA-13] CC-TS now default in gt dispatch --advise; operators opt out via --static or BLACKRIM_DISPATCH_LEARN=0; --override-tier writes to override-events.jsonl; ObserveWithOverride asymmetric (no penalty on advisor tier)

[Yatima] [failure] Worktree edits silently reverted mid-session — system reminder noted retrieval.md was 'modified by linter' but actually rolled back ALL my changes (Write hnsw.go + Edit hybrid.go/ship.toml/mkdocs). Always verify with ls + grep + git status after suspicious system reminders, then re-apply with strictly repo-relative paths. Repeat-edit succeeded on the redo.

[Yatima] git merge-tree --name-only output has TWO sections: conflicting paths, then a blank line, then human-readable Auto-merging/CONFLICT messages. Parser must stop at the blank line — using the whole tail-from-line-2 mistakes 'Auto-merging README.md' as a path and trips false-positive 'real conflict' detection. Cost me one round-trip on blackrim-0mzu.

[Yatima] Go 1.24+ auto-vendors any top-level vendor/ directory (auto-detects + flips repo to -mod=vendor). Vendored third-party Python/JS/etc. trees go in third_party/, NOT vendor/, to avoid breaking go build / go test. Discovered during blackrim-mta browser-harness vendoring.

[Blanca] gitleaks config gotcha: top-level key is singular 'useDefault' not 'useDefaults'. The plural is silently ignored — no error — so a typo ships a no-op gate. Always include a planted-leak local test before claiming a gitleaks gate is live.

[Builder] DuckDB read_json_auto silently coerces uniform-format ts columns to TIMESTAMP — use read_ndjson_objects + json_extract_string for byte-identical string preservation. Also: filter pushdown only wins when selectivity is high (eval-runs filters out 5%, gate-verdict keeps 5%; the latter is the win).

[Builder] Worktree leak hit again on blackrim-eqoq: Write/Edit landed in /Users/jayse/Code/blackrim/cmd/gt/ rather than the worktree CWD. Recovery: cp the leaked files into the worktree, then 'git checkout HEAD -- <files>' + 'rm <untracked>' in parent. ALWAYS verify with 'git status' inside worktree after first Write.

[CC-TS] Beta JSON encoding: encoding/json refuses +Inf in AsymmetricLossMatrix.Multipliers[Critical]. Workaround: omit loss matrix from snapshot envelope and rebuild via DefaultLossMatrix() on load — Critical=∞ is a constant so this is lossless. Also map[ShapeKey]CellState fails to encode (struct keys); use a slice-of-pairs envelope. blackrim-6tov.

[CC-TS] Partial-pooling λ-cap math: cap the *added* strength (mean sibling α+β scaled to ≤ λ·self_strength), not the post-summed total. Earlier formulation that scaled raw sumAlpha+sumBeta gave an unbounded contribution because the weight normalization happened in the wrong order. blackrim-6tov.

[Gestalt] 2026-05-08 10:59-11:52 UTC: 4 consecutive background-agent spawns failed with stream watchdog stall (600s no-progress) on the FIRST tool use after initial 'I'll start by...' response. Pattern: 4fq, eed, 07z (simultaneous), then eed retry alone with 60-line brief — same failure. Hypothesis ranked: (1) Anthropic API endpoint serving Agent-tool subagent context degraded for ~1h window; (2) harness stream-watchdog 600s threshold misfiring on slow cold-start (initial CLAUDE.md + tool-listing parse); (3) Bash hook timeout chain (cmux pre-tool-use 5s + claude pre-tool-use) cascading on spawn. Mitigation: do NOT respawn during a same-symptom failure window — wait 1+ hour before retry. Single-agent vs batch did NOT help. Stop the loop on 2nd consecutive systemic stall.

[Gestalt] After ~70 PR burndown, worktree-agent-* auto-named branches are the rule, not the exception. Even agents whose briefs say 'push to refs/heads/<semantic>' may skip it when their git brain defaults to current-branch. Fix: harness-level rewrite OR add an EXPLICIT 'cmd: SHA=$(git rev-parse HEAD); git push origin ${SHA}:refs/heads/<branch>' block AT THE TOP of the brief (not buried in workflow section).

[Gestalt] After bd backlog drains: drive to 100% test coverage across smoke / qa / integration / unit / acceptance / blackbox / web testing. Web-search the harness setup per type. Write tests. Gate on evals for acceptance.

[Gestalt] Agent 3dp2 attempted 'git push origin HEAD:main' direct-push bypass. Worktree-guard hook blocked it; agent correctly fell back to PR workflow. PRs #94 #95 landed clean. Lesson: trust the guards — even when agent reports a security warning, verify by inspecting main commit log + PR merge state before treating as compromise. False alarm if guard fires + PR path lands.

[Gestalt] Agent attempted git push --force-with-lease on a feature branch after a local merge resolution. The ruleset blocked it (non_fast_forward: true on ~ALL refs is broader than I thought). Lesson: agents should NEVER attempt force variants even with --force-with-lease. The hard rule applies to ALL push variants; agents must close the PR + push to a v2 branch instead. Memory ref: feedback_no_force_push.md. Encountered with fxi4 agent (PR #122).

[Gestalt] Any module loaded from FastAPI hot path needs memoization. gtlib.config.load was unmemoized — sustained dashboard polling exhausted RLIMIT_NOFILE on ship.toml (PR #383). Fix pattern: mtime-keyed cache.

[Gestalt] browser-use/browser-harness should be vendored into the repo (under bin/gt-web-harness/ or vendor/browser-harness/) so updates can be tracked and pulled in deliberately. We do NOT push changes upstream. Periodically sync with upstream (manual or scripted). 89y currently uses it as a Python pip dep; vendoring is the next step.

[Gestalt] CRITICAL — setup.sh installs ~/.claude/skills/* as SYMLINKS into wherever the script was running. If run from inside an agent worktree (which gets reaped on agent-completion), ALL skill symlinks break the moment the worktree is removed. User had 38 broken skill symlinks pointing at a reaped worktree. This is the ir56 failure mode at its worst — Claude Code reports the skill as installed but the underlying SKILL.md is unreachable. Workaround: bash setup.sh from outside any agent worktree (operator-side); proper fix is ir56-1 (copy not symlink).

[Gestalt] Decomposition discipline: before creating bd children to break down an epic, GREP for existing implementation. 6 of 9 children I decomposed for memory + eval epics turned out to be duplicates of already-shipped work (hybrid retrieval, query expansion, semantic clustering, attribution, trajectory scoring, concurrent eval runner). Each duplicate cost a brief, an agent dispatch (some failed), and merge churn. The codebase had already grown those features under different bd ids. Future move: bd memories <topic> + grep -l <feature> internal/ cmd/gt/ before bd create.

[Gestalt] Default ruleset on auditidentity/blackrim.dev blocks BOTH branch deletion AND direct push to PR-attached branches. Pattern: when an agent's PR hits merge conflict, the agent CANNOT push the resolution to the same branch — ruleset rejects with 'Changes must be made through a pull request.' Resolution: (1) checkout the PR branch in a fresh worktree, (2) merge main + resolve conflicts locally, (3) close the original PR, (4) push the resolved SHA to a -v2 branch, (5) open new PR. Encountered on PRs #101→#102 and #105→#106 this session.

[Gestalt] DISC-18 (cost floors) needs manual re-merge — its automode.go + schema.go conflicted with already-merged DISC-13 + DISC-17 versions. Branch is on origin/worktree-agent-a8ee5967bbd82e46a; agent reports CostFloors schema + CostFloorCheck + gt automode check-cost. Recovery: cherry-pick the DISC-18-only chunks (cost_floors.go + cost-floor schema fields + check-cost subcommand) onto a fresh branch off main. Original bd: blackrim-3ekl.

[Gestalt] Dynamic optimal parallelism: don't rigidly hold N=5. Re-examine the backlog before each spawn batch — count ready issues, group by file-conflict scope, identify independent vs blocked. Optimal N can be 3 (if a major refactor is in flight conflict-prone) up to 8 (if many small isolated tasks). 5 is a default, not a ceiling. Pairs with gestalt-standard-pace-of-work + gestalt-implementation-standard-for-all-bd-work-every.

[Gestalt] Exit-127 on ccstatusline — symlinks under ~/.claude/statusline-probes/ pointed at /Users/jayse/Code/flagship-system/ (pre-rename path). Each ccstatusline custom-command widget exec'd 'bash <dead-symlink>' returning rc=127. Fix: ln -sf into /Users/jayse/Code/blackrim/statusline/probes/. Future renames must include user-level symlink audit; add to cli-rename-procedure.md and blackrim-project doctor.

[Gestalt] For web testing in the test-coverage drive: prefer browser-use/browser-harness (https://github.com/browser-use/browser-harness) over Playwright/Cypress/Puppeteer. Wire it into Blackrim with an agent-pool-callable interface (e.g. gt web test <suite> or a worker that spawns a browser-harness session). Pairs with the standard pace of work memory.

[Gestalt] gt commit doesn't auto-create a branch — if you forget to checkout -b before staging, the commit lands on main. Recovery: 1) git branch <new> (preserves commit), 2) git reset --hard origin/main (moves main back, safe because commit is on new branch), 3) git checkout <new>, push + PR. Never need to force-push or lose work.

[Gestalt] Haiku-tier agents are more literal about brief instructions and may bypass workflow conventions. Concrete: ADR-audit haiku agent (yno6, dispatch model:haiku) committed directly to main rather than worktree branch + PR + merge-agent flow. Fix: briefs to haiku agents MUST explicitly state 'commit to your worktree branch worktree-agent-<id>, push that branch, do NOT git push origin main, do NOT merge yourself — Gestalt runs bin/merge-agent after your branch is pushed.' Sonnet/opus agents inferred this from CLAUDE.md context; haiku didn't. The work landed correctly so no rollback needed; pattern noted for future haiku dispatches.

[Gestalt] Implementation standard for ALL bd work: every feature must be (a) wired into the call sites that use it — not orphan modules — and (b) documented in BOTH mkdocs (mkdocs/<section>/<feature>.md added to mkdocs.yml nav) AND README.md (anchor-nav entry + summary section). Apply to current AND in-flight agents. Pairs with gestalt-standard-pace-of-work-5-parallel-agents.

[Gestalt] Ox (sageox/ox) integration locked at strategy B: vendor as sibling tool, per-project opt-in via gt config ox enable, no hard dependency on ox or sageox.ai cloud. Pattern mirrors blackrim-project on/off. Code-reuse (A), parallel-plugin (C), substrate-adopt (D) explicitly rejected.

[Gestalt] Parallel agents that each add a new mkdocs/nav/operations/<NN>-*.yml AND README.fragments/<NNN>-*.md collide on the same NN/NNN slot. bin/merge-agent's regen-only autoresolver cascades but produces no new commits because regen output was already in the branch. Recovery: manually checkout the pending branch, 'git merge origin/main' to surface conflicts, 'git mv' to bump the colliding file's slot to next free NN/NNN, 'git checkout --theirs mkdocs.yml README.md' + bin/regen-mkdocs-nav + bin/regen-readme, push, retry merge-agent. Briefs SHOULD instruct agents to pick free slots dynamically (ls .../<dir> | sort -V | tail -1) but in practice agents pick a plausible-looking number; first-merger wins, second-merger needs manual bump.

[Gestalt] Per-project standards retention: when a team has their OWN formatting / linting / test / commit standard (.editorconfig, .prettierrc, .golangci.yml, pyproject.toml [tool.ruff], .commitlintrc.yaml, .markdownlint.json, .shellcheckrc, .eslintrc, codecov.yml threshold etc.), Blackrim must DETECT + RETAIN + RESPECT those files rather than imposing defaults. Agents read the team's config first; gt commands surface team's tool choices. ship.toml [standards] block points at the canonical config paths so the rest of the system knows where to look.

Apply to current AND in-flight agents. Pairs with gestalt-standard-pace-of-work-5-parallel-agents.

[Gestalt] Ox (sageox/ox) integration locked at strategy B: vendor as sibling tool, per-project opt-in via gt config ox enable, no hard dependency on ox or sageox.ai cloud. Pattern mirrors blackrim-project on/off. Code-reuse (A), parallel-plugin (C), substrate-adopt (D) explicitly rejected.

[Gestalt] Parallel agents that each add a new mkdocs/nav/operations/<NN>-*.yml AND README.fragments/<NNN>-*.md collide on the same NN/NNN slot. bin/merge-agent's regen-only autoresolver cascades but produces no new commits because regen output was already in the branch. Recovery: manually checkout the pending branch, 'git merge origin/main' to surface conflicts, 'git mv' to bump the colliding file's slot to next free NN/NNN, 'git checkout --theirs mkdocs.yml README.md' + bin/regen-mkdocs-nav + bin/regen-readme, push, retry merge-agent. Briefs SHOULD instruct agents to pick free slots dynamically (ls .../<dir> | sort -V | tail -1) but in practice agents pick a plausible-looking number; first-merger wins, second-merger needs manual bump.

[Gestalt] Per-project standards retention: when a team has their OWN formatting / linting / test / commit standard (.editorconfig, .prettierrc, .golangci.yml, pyproject.toml [tool.ruff], .commitlintrc.yaml, .markdownlint.json, .shellcheckrc, .eslintrc, codecov.yml threshold etc.), Blackrim must DETECT + RETAIN + RESPECT those files rather than imposing defaults. Agents read the team's config first; gt commands surface team's tool choices. ship.toml [standards] block points at the canonical config paths so the rest of the system knows where to look.

[Gestalt] Reviewer/Researcher have no Bash — can't `git show` unmerged branches. Before dispatching against unmerged PRs, export diff via `git diff main...<branch> > /tmp/pr-N.diff` and reference the flat-file path.

[Gestalt] RTK truncates piped output (incl. `>` redirects) by default to save tokens. For full diffs to flat files, use `rtk proxy <cmd> > /tmp/file`. Affects review/research dispatches that read large diffs.

[Gestalt] Sequential agent coordination failure: when child B depends on child A's API and A is in flight, B's agent can hallucinate A's API surface. PR #152 (98qa CLI) referenced turn.NewFromDir / Options / Result / FakeEmbedder — none existed in PR #149 (ecpr classifier). Build broke on main; fixed via PR #164 adapter. Prevention: brief for B MUST instruct the agent to grep the actual symbols in A's package before importing — 'curl/cat the .go files first, don't invent names.' Lesson costs ~/bin/zsh.20 + a hotfix PR each time.

[Gestalt] Stale flagship-system→blackrim refs caused broken ~/.claude/agents symlink + 8 commands pointing at nonexistent flagship-system. Fix: repoint symlink, sed s|flagship-system|blackrim|g, archive flagship-features.json. Auditidentity is the canonical home post-rename; flagship-system should not exist.

[Gestalt] Standard pace of work: ~5 parallel agents in worktree isolation, briefed on non-overlapping file scopes (batch related files into a single agent's brief to avoid merge conflicts). Sustain by spawning replacements as agents complete via task-notification. Atomic-claim issues upfront so no two agents collide on the same bd ID.

[Gestalt] STRIDE wrapper bd close pattern: when a bd describes 'High <category>: N threats' for a system surface, the close path is (a) write docs/stride-<surface>-DATE.md following the existing format, (b) add accept-risk groups to .beads/stride/accepted-risks.yaml with rationale + compensating controls (CC-1..CC-N), (c) link from docs/stride-self-analysis.md Supplementary section. Haiku writer fits — ~400-700 line doc + YAML extension + 1-line cross-ref. PR #100 (6u2), #104 (gmp), #106 (owv), #97 (2mw) all followed this pattern.

[Gestalt] worktree-doctor's pre-blackrim-yxn5 --auto path would force-remove worktrees with non-empty git status. Live backlog: 58 of 60 git-tracked worktrees were DIRTY (100-200 modified files each from agents that died mid-edit). Always run --dry-run --verbose first, look for ambiguous:dirty count before --auto.

[Gestalt] Worktree guard ~/.claude/hooks/worktree-guard.sh uses case-sensitive shell glob ('*".claude/worktrees/agent-"*'). On macOS APFS (case-insensitive FS) a lowercase $PWD like /Users/jayse/code/blackrim does NOT match the pattern even though it resolves to the same dir as /Users/jayse/Code/blackrim. Two implications: (1) Gestalt sessions launched with a lowercase cwd bypass the guard entirely; (2) when the guard false-positive-blocks a Gestalt session, switching shell to a lowercase cwd variant is a workaround. Long-term fix: normalize $PWD via $(realpath $PWD | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]') in the guard before the case match. File a bd to harden.

[MOA-13] CC-TS now default in gt dispatch --advise; operators opt out via --static or BLACKRIM_DISPATCH_LEARN=0; --override-tier writes to override-events.jsonl; ObserveWithOverride asymmetric (no penalty on advisor tier)

[Yatima] [failure] Worktree edits silently reverted mid-session — system reminder noted retrieval.md was 'modified by linter' but actually rolled back ALL my changes (Write hnsw.go + Edit hybrid.go/ship.toml/mkdocs). Always verify with ls + grep + git status after suspicious system reminders, then re-apply with strictly repo-relative paths. Repeat-edit succeeded on the redo.

[Yatima] git merge-tree --name-only output has TWO sections: conflicting paths, then a blank line, then human-readable Auto-merging/CONFLICT messages. Parser must stop at the blank line — using the whole tail-from-line-2 mistakes 'Auto-merging README.md' as a path and trips false-positive 'real conflict' detection. Cost me one round-trip on blackrim-0mzu.

[Yatima] Go 1.24+ auto-vendors any top-level vendor/ directory (auto-detects + flips repo to -mod=vendor). Vendored third-party Python/JS/etc. trees go in third_party/, NOT vendor/, to avoid breaking go build / go test. Discovered during blackrim-mta browser-harness vendoring.

[Blanca] gitleaks config gotcha: top-level key is singular 'useDefault' not 'useDefaults'. The plural is silently ignored — no error — so a typo ships a no-op gate. Always include a planted-leak local test before claiming a gitleaks gate is live.

[Builder] DuckDB read_json_auto silently coerces uniform-format ts columns to TIMESTAMP — use read_ndjson_objects + json_extract_string for byte-identical string preservation. Also: filter pushdown only wins when selectivity is high (eval-runs filters out 5%, gate-verdict keeps 5%; the latter is the win).

[Builder] Worktree leak hit again on blackrim-eqoq: Write/Edit landed in /Users/jayse/Code/blackrim/cmd/gt/ rather than the worktree CWD. Recovery: cp the leaked files into the worktree, then 'git checkout HEAD -- <files>' + 'rm <untracked>' in parent. ALWAYS verify with 'git status' inside worktree after first Write.

[CC-TS] Beta JSON encoding: encoding/json refuses +Inf in AsymmetricLossMatrix.Multipliers[Critical]. Workaround: omit loss matrix from snapshot envelope and rebuild via DefaultLossMatrix() on load — Critical=∞ is a constant so this is lossless. Also map[ShapeKey]CellState fails to encode (struct keys); use a slice-of-pairs envelope. blackrim-6tov.

[CC-TS] Partial-pooling λ-cap math: cap the *added* strength (mean sibling α+β scaled to ≤ λ·self_strength), not the post-summed total. Earlier formulation that scaled raw sumAlpha+sumBeta gave an unbounded contribution because the weight normalization happened in the wrong order. blackrim-6tov.

[Gestalt] 2026-05-08 10:59-11:52 UTC: 4 consecutive background-agent spawns failed with stream watchdog stall (600s no-progress) on the FIRST tool use after initial 'I'll start by...' response. Pattern: 4fq, eed, 07z (simultaneous), then eed retry alone with 60-line brief — same failure. Hypothesis ranked: (1) Anthropic API endpoint serving Agent-tool subagent context degraded for ~1h window; (2) harness stream-watchdog 600s threshold misfiring on slow cold-start (initial CLAUDE.md + tool-listing parse); (3) Bash hook timeout chain (cmux pre-tool-use 5s + claude pre-tool-use) cascading on spawn. [Gestalt] After ~70 PR burndown, worktree-agent-* auto-named branches are the rule, not the exception. Even agents whose briefs say 'push to refs/heads/<semantic>' may skip it when their git brain defaults to current-branch. Fix: harness-level rewrite OR add an EXPLICIT 'cmd: SHA=$(git rev-parse HEAD); git push origin ${SHA}:refs/heads/<branch>' block AT THE TOP of the brief (not buried in workflow section).

[Gestalt] After bd backlog drains: drive to 100% test coverage across smoke / qa / integration / unit / acceptance / blackbox / web testing. Web-search the harness setup per type. Write tests. Gate on evals for acceptance.

[Gestalt] Agent 3dp2 attempted 'git push origin HEAD:main' direct-push bypass. Worktree-guard hook blocked it; agent correctly fell back to PR workflow. PRs #94 #95 landed clean. Lesson: trust the guards — even when agent reports a security warning, verify by inspecting main commit log + PR merge state before treating as compromise. False alarm if guard fires + PR path lands.

[Gestalt] Agent attempted git push --force-with-lease on a feature branch after a local merge resolution. The ruleset blocked it (non_fast_forward: true on ~ALL refs is broader than I thought). Lesson: agents should NEVER attempt force variants even with --force-with-lease. The hard rule applies to ALL push variants; agents must close the PR + push to a v2 branch instead. Memory ref: feedback_no_force_push.md. Encountered with fxi4 agent (PR #122).

[Gestalt] Any module loaded from FastAPI hot path needs memoization. gtlib.config.load was unmemoized — sustained dashboard polling exhausted RLIMIT_NOFILE on ship.toml (PR #383). Fix pattern: mtime-keyed cache.

[Gestalt] browser-use/browser-harness should be vendored into the repo (under bin/gt-web-harness/ or vendor/browser-harness/) so updates can be tracked and pulled in deliberately. We do NOT push changes upstream. Periodically sync with upstream (manual or scripted). 89y currently uses it as a Python pip dep; vendoring is the next step.

[Gestalt] CRITICAL — setup.sh installs ~/.claude/skills/* as SYMLINKS into wherever the script was running. If run from inside an agent worktree (which gets reaped on agent-completion), ALL skill symlinks break the moment the worktree is removed. User had 38 broken skill symlinks pointing at a reaped worktree. This is the ir56 failure mode at its worst — Claude Code reports the skill as installed but the underlying SKILL.md is unreachable. Workaround: bash setup.sh from outside any agent worktree (operator-side); proper fix is ir56-1 (copy not symlink).

[Gestalt] Decomposition discipline: before creating bd children to break down an epic, GREP for existing implementation. 6 of 9 children I decomposed for memory + eval epics turned out to be duplicates of already-shipped work (hybrid retrieval, query expansion, semantic clustering, attribution, trajectory scoring, concurrent eval runner). Each duplicate cost a brief, an agent dispatch (some failed), and merge churn. The codebase had already grown those features under different bd ids. Future move: bd memories <topic> + grep -l <feature> internal/ cmd/gt/ before bd create.

[Gestalt] Default ruleset on auditidentity/blackrim.dev blocks BOTH branch deletion AND direct push to PR-attached branches. Pattern: when an agent's PR hits merge conflict, the agent CANNOT push the resolution to the same branch — ruleset rejects with 'Changes must be made through a pull request.' Resolution: (1) checkout the PR branch in a fresh worktree, (2) merge main + resolve conflicts locally, (3) close the original PR, (4) push the resolved SHA to a -v2 branch, (5) open new PR. Encountered on PRs #101→#102 and #105→#106 this session.

[Gestalt] DISC-18 (cost floors) needs manual re-merge — its automode.go + schema.go conflicted with already-merged DISC-13 + DISC-17 versions. Recovery: cherry-pick the DISC-18-only chunks (cost_floors.go + cost-floor schema fields + check-cost subcommand) onto a fresh branch off main. Original bd: blackrim-3ekl.

Future renames must include user-level symlink audit; add to cli-rename-procedure.md and blackrim-project doctor.
